evidence: Person missing recommended: jobTitle, sameAs, hasCredential

Author has no sameAs URLs (should link LinkedIn + at least one other profile)
Author has no jobTitle or description — credentials not stated
No first-party data signals ("we tested", "our data shows", "we analyzed"). Add at least one concrete observation
Only 3/4 human signals present (author+LinkedIn=false, first-party data=false, original visual=true, 3+ citations=true). High AI-content-flag risk
Specific human voice 5/10 — Flashes of voice ('desert humbles every calendar') fight with committee-written framing sentences.
Unexpected phrasings 6/10 — Some vivid lines ('quietly rotting through spring') are undermined by generic SEO boilerplate elsewhere.
Specific citations 6/10 — Has a date (19 March 2026), price (AED 1,200), named bodies (DHA, MOHAP), but few specific names, clinics, or case details.
Intro earns attention 5/10 — The hook line exists but is buried after duplicate title, date stamps, and a generic TL;DR.

specificity 6/10 — Several passages cite 'behaviour science research' and 'evidence' without naming the study, year, finding, or statistic. Phrases like 'notable detail,' 'more effective than generic planning,' and 'small, regionally aware changes can make Q2 more manageable' are abstract claims floating without concrete anchors. The post does well with named locations (JBR, Al Safa Park, 28°C, AED 1,200) and specific tests, but the evidence passages consistently vague out. Fixing the citation sentences to include actual numbers or study details would lift this axis to 8+.
